_source: Standard-normal quantiles Phi^-1(0.95)=1.6448536269514722 and Phi^-1(0.99)=2.3263478740408408 (published). ParametricVaR(mu,sigma,c)=sigma*z_c-mu: (0,1,0.95)=1.6448536269514722; (0.001,0.02,0.99)=0.02*2.3263478740408408-0.001=0.045526957480816816. CornishFisherVaR with skew=0,exKurt=0 reduces exactly to Phi^-1(c): (0,1,0.99)=2.3263478740408408. Non-normal case (0,1,skew=-0.5,exKurt=2.0,0.99): lower-tail z=-2.3263478740408408; Cornish-Fisher expansion z_cf = z + (z^2-1)/6*S + (z^3-3z)/24*K - (2z^3-5z)/36*S^2 = -3.0674966; VaR=-z_cf=3.0674966 (hand-derived, tol loosened for the multi-term expansion). ParametricCVaR standard-normal ES = phi(z_c)/(1-c): c=0.95 -> 2.0627128, c=0.99 -> 2.6652142 (published normal expected-shortfall values).

cases.description: CornishFisherVaR reduces to ParametricVaR when skew=exKurt=0
CornishFisherVaR negative skew, fat tail widens VaR above Gaussian
